title: Write-only properties should be avoided
description: Write-only properties should not be usedAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellpitfallWhy is this an issue?Properties with only setters are confusing and counterintuitive. Instead, a property getter should be added if possible, or the property should be replaced with a setter method.Noncompliant code exampleclass Program { public int Foo //Non-Compliant { set { // ... some code ..
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: Write-only property detected. Properties should have both getter and setter, or use a setter method instead.

never_trigger_on: Properties with both get and set accessors
logic.reasoning: Write-only properties are confusing and counterintuitive. If a value can be set, it should typically be readable as well. If only writing is needed, a SetXxx() method is clearer. The negative lookahead (?![^}]*\bget\b) ensures we only match properties that have 'set' without 'get'.

should_not_trigger: public int Foo { get { return _foo; } set { _foo = value; } }
